title: Highly-branched cyclic dextrins as malodor control agents
abstract: The present invention relates to a fabric conditioning composition comprising a fabric softening agent, a highly-branched cyclic dextrin, and water, to a fabric refresher composition comprising at least one highly-branched cyclic dextrin in an amount of at least 0.01 wt.-%, based on the total weight of the fabric refresher composition, and to a liquid laundry detergent composition comprising at least one highly-branched cyclic dextrin in an amount of at least 0.01 wt.% based on the total weight of the liquid laundry detergent composition, as well as to a method for laundering fabric, to a method for reducing or eliminating malodor and to the use of the highly-branched cyclic dextrin for reducing or eliminating malodors.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . A fabric conditioning composition comprising: a fabric softening agent; a highly-branched cyclic dextrin; and water. 2. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 1 , wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin has an average molecular weight of about 100 Daltons to about 5000 kilo Daltons. 3. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 2, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin has an average molecular weight of about 500 Daltons to about 3000 kilo Daltons. 4. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 1 , wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 1 .0 wt.-% to about 8.0 wt.- %. 5. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 4, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 2.0 wt.-% to about 4.0 wt.- %. 6. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 1 , wherein the fabric softening agent comprises a quaternary ammonium ester compound. 7. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 6, wherein the fabric softening agent is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 4.0 wt.-% to about 15.0 wt.-%. 8. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 1 , wherein the water is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 75 wt.-% to about 95 wt.-%. 9. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 1 , further comprising a rheology polymer. 10. The fabric conditioning composition of claim 9, wherein the rheology polymer is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 0.001 wt.-% to about 0.5 wt.-%. 11 . A method for laundering fabric, comprising: 32 

 applying a detergent composition to the fabric; rinsing the detergent composition from the fabric; after rinsing the detergent composition from the fabric, applying a fabric conditioning composition to the fabric, wherein the fabric conditioning composition comprises a fabric softening agent, a highly-branched cyclic dextrin, and water; and rinsing the fabric conditioning composition from the fabric. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the highly- branched cyclic dextrin has an average molecular weight of about 100 Daltons to about 5000 kilo Daltons. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the highly- branched cyclic dextrin has an average molecular weight of about 500 Daltons to about 3000 kilo Daltons. 14. The method of claim 11 , wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the highly- branched cyclic dextrin is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 1.0 wt.-% to about 8.0 wt.-%. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the highly- branched cyclic dextrin is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 2.0 wt.-% to about 4.0 wt.-%. 16. The method of claim 11 , wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the fabric softening agent comprises a quaternary ammonium ester compound. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the fabric softening agent is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 4.0 wt.-% to about 15.0 wt.-%. 18. The method of claim 11 , wherein in the fabric conditioning composition, the water is present in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 75 wt.-% to about 95 wt.-%. 19. The method of claim 1 1 , further comprising a rheology polymer in the fabric conditioning composition in an amount of about 0.001 wt.-% to about 0.5 wt.-%. 33 20. A fabric conditioning composition comprising: a fabric softening agent comprising a quaternary ammonium ester compound in an amount of about 4.0 wt.-% to about 15.0 wt.-%; a highly-branched cyclic dextrin having an average molecular weight of about 500 Daltons to about 3000 kilo Daltons in an amount of about 2.0 wt.-% to about 4.0 wt.-%; and water in an amount of about 85 wt.-% to about 92 wt.-%. 21 . A fabric refresher composition comprising at least one highly-branched cyclic dextrin in an amount of at least 0.01 wt.%, based on the total weight of the fabric refresher composition. 22. The fabric refresher composition of claim 21 , wherein the fabric refresher composition comprises the at least one highly-branched cyclic dextrin in an amount of from 0.01 to 30 wt.%, preferably in an amount of from 0.01 to 25 wt.%, and most preferably in an amount of from 0.01 to 5 wt.%, based on the total weight of the fabric refresher composition. 23. The fabric refresher composition of claim 21 or 22, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin has an average molecular weight of about 100 Da to about 5000 kDa, preferably about 500 Da to about 3000 kDa. 24. The fabric refresher composition of any one of claims 21 to 23, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is maltodextrin (CAS: 9050-36-6). 25. The fabric refresher composition of any one of claims 21 to 24, wherein the fabric refresher composition further comprises at least one perfume or a perfume composition. 26. The fabric refresher composition of any one of claims 21 to 25, wherein the fabric refresher composition is a liquid fabric refresher composition, preferably a water-based liquid fabric refresher composition. 27. The fabric refresher composition of any one of claims 21 to 26, wherein the fabric refresher composition is a malodor removing spray, in particular for malodors caused by pets. 28. A method for reducing or eliminating malodor, preferably malodor caused by pets, comprising the following step: 

 applying in at least one method step the fabric refresher composition according to any one of claims 21 to 27 to a fabric to be treated, wherein the fabric refresher composition is preferably sprayed onto the fabric. 29. Use of a highly-branched cyclic dextrin for reducing or eliminating malodors of a fabric. 30. The use according to claim 29, wherein the malodors are caused by pets. 31 The use according to claim 29 or 30, wherein the fabric is a textile, carpet or furniture. 32. The use according to any one of claims 29 to 31 , wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is used in combination with at least one perfume or a perfume composition, preferably in a fabric refresher composition such as a malodor removing spray. 33. A liquid laundry detergent composition comprising at least one highly-branched cyclic dextrin in an amount of at least 0.01 wt.% based on the total weight of the liquid laundry detergent composition. 34. The liquid laundry detergent composition according to claim 33, wherein the at least one highly-branched cyclic dextrin is comprised in an amount of from 0.01 to 30 wt.%, preferably from 0.01 to 25 wt.%, and most preferably from 0.01 to 5 wt.%, based on the total weight of the liquid laundry detergent composition. 35. The liquid laundry detergent composition according to claim 33 or 34, wherein the highly- branched cyclic dextrin has an average molecular weight of about 100 Da to about 5000 kDa, preferably about 500 Da to about 3000 kDa. 36. The liquid laundry detergent composition according to any one of claims 33 to 35, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is maltodextrin (CAS: 9050-36-6). 37. The liquid laundry detergent composition according to any one of claims 33 to 36, wherein the liquid laundry detergent composition further comprises at least one perfume or a perfume composition. 38. A method for reducing or eliminating malodor, preferably malodor caused by sweat, comprising the following step: 

 contacting in at least one method step the liquid laundry detergent composition according to any one of claims 33 to 37 with a fabric to be treated, wherein the contacting preferably occurs in a laundry washing process, preferably in an automated washing machine. 39. The use according to claim 29, wherein the malodors are caused by sweat. 40. The use according to claim 29 or 39, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is maltodextrin (CAS: 9050-36-6). 41 . The use according to any one of claims 29, 39 or 40, wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is used in combination with at least one perfume or a perfume composition, preferably in a liquid laundry detergent composition. 42. The use according to any one of claims 29, 39, 40 or 41 , wherein the highly-branched cyclic dextrin is used within a washing process, preferably a machine washing process or hand washing. 36
